other_observers: On 1/8/12 with Mary Cay Burger and many other observers. On 2/21/12 with Mary Cay Burger and her wildlife biologist brother John from Spokane and a couple other birders.; Do not remember the original observer. Many posts by Joe Roller and others on seeing the owl in this exact spot through Jan and at least through our sighting and photos on 2/21/12.
specific_location: Viewed and photographed from 138th Ave just ~1/4 mi west of Harvest Rd on 1/8/12 and 2/21/12
description: Typcial ~23" tall juvenile Snowy Owl coloration: white face with eyes and bill visible, no ears, rotund body, brown barring on breast, back, wings, and top and back of head.Behaviors: Juvenile Snowy Owl was resting ~100 yds from 138th Ave, Adams Co., CO in ~8"-tall weedy strip of pipeline land that was parallell to the road and the high point of the surrounding area. It appeared to be resting and was swivelling its head around to view the area. It sat in the same spot for ~1 hr and was viewed by others.Call: None heardPlumage: juvenile
photographer: Photographer: Kayleen A Niyo, Kayleen A Niyo
